title: NZ in December with Kids — What to Expect (Weather, Crowds, Costs)
meta_desc: Discover what to expect when travelling NZ in December with kids, including weather, crowds and costs.
intro: December is one of the busiest months to travel in New Zealand, with the Christmas school holidays bringing large crowds to popular destinations. If you're planning a trip with kids, it's essential to know what to expect. With temperatures ranging from 18-25°C, December is an excellent time to enjoy the beach and outdoor activities.

sections.heading: Weather in December
sections.body: December is summer in New Zealand, with warm temperatures and high humidity. The average temperature in Auckland is 23°C, while in Queenstown it's around 18°C. Make sure to pack sunscreen, hats, and light clothing for your trip.

sections.heading: Peak Season Reality
list: Expect large crowds at popular destinations like Rotorua, Lake Taupo, and the Coromandel Peninsula
Book accommodations and activities well in advance to avoid high prices and availability issues
Be prepared for long drive times and traffic congestion, especially during holidays and weekends

sections.heading: Beach Destinations
sections.body: New Zealand has many beautiful beaches, but some are more crowded than others during peak season. Popular spots like Mount Maunganui, Raglan, and the Bay of Islands can get very busy, while more secluded beaches like those in the Catlins or the East Coast may offer a more relaxed atmosphere.

sections.heading: What to Book Early
list: Accommodation in popular destinations, with prices starting from $150 per night for a family room
Activities like bungee jumping or ski lessons, with prices ranging from $100-$300 per person
Car rentals, with prices starting from $60 per day for a compact vehicle

cta.text: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator

sections.heading: More Information
sections.body: For more information on travelling in New Zealand, check out our destinations page for guides on the best places to visit, including the West Coast, Abel Tasman National Park, and the geothermal wonders of Rotorua. You can also explore our campervans page for rental options and tips on how to plan the perfect road trip.
